scenario.scale: IT service provider for home office, municipal workplaces and medical practice environments, 95 employees
scenario.context: During an internal alerting drill for evening and weekend incidents, outdated contact paths, unclear on-call substitutes and a patchy escalation path became visible.
scenario.mission: Define contact status, on-call coverage, escalation sequence and completion evidence for alerting.

source.note: Based on a failed alerting drill, old mobile numbers in the on-call list, a no-longer-valid hotline for an infrastructure partner and the requirement to put on-call and escalation status in order by Friday.

station.department: Operations, emergency coordination and service management
station.profile: Alerting, on-call coverage, escalation and completion evidence
constraints: Contact path, role and substitute must be clearly and currently documented per alerting level
Hotline, on-call coverage and internal escalation path must be practically usable, not just formally present
By Friday a readable closure with updated contact status, test result and open residual points is required

customerRequest.subject: Follow up alerting drill for evening operations and on-call readiness cleanly
customerRequest.body:
Good day,

during our drill for evening and weekend incidents several parts did not work cleanly: two mobile numbers in the on-call list were outdated, the substitute for platform operations was not clearly named, and the stored hotline of an infrastructure partner is no longer valid according to feedback.

Please prepare a reliable status by Friday: which contact paths must be corrected immediately, how the escalation sequence for evening incidents is built cleanly, which substitutes are binding and which evidence we need for a traceable closure.

Kind regards
Operations

contentBlocks.text: SecuBridge tests alerting for incidents outside daytime operations involving on-call staff, substitutes and external partners. The current drill shows not a single defect, but multiple breaks in contact lists, substitution logic and escalation sequence.

rows: No clearly named channel if first contacts are unreachable
Updated contact sequence, named substitutes, test evidence and open residual points

contentBlocks.text: The goal is not a general emergency document, but a concretely usable alerting status for real evening or weekend incidents.

questions.title: Points to clarify immediately
questions.prompt: Which points must be clarified or corrected first after the alerting drill? Several points can be mandatory.

options.text: Update outdated mobile numbers in the on-call list and recheck the contact path.
options.rationale: Without reachable first contacts, every alerting logic remains on paper.

options.text: Define a binding substitute for platform operations with their own reachability.
options.rationale: An unclear substitute tears the escalation sequence at a critical spot.

options.text: Correct the invalid hotline data of the infrastructure partner or secure an alternative contact.
options.rationale: External escalation must be actually usable in an emergency.

options.text: Define a fallback channel in case first contact and substitute are unreachable.
options.rationale: Without a fallback level, the alert flow stops at the first gap.

options.text: Contact lists can remain unchanged as long as it is known internally who usually responds.
options.rationale: Informal knowledge does not replace reliable alerting outside daytime operations.

options.text: A successful drill is not needed if the team calendar already shows on-call coverage.
options.rationale: Calendar entries do not prove that contact path and escalation work in practice.

feedback.correct: The first points to clarify after the drill were correctly prioritized.
feedback.wrong: Focus on current reachability, clear substitute, external contact path and a real fallback.

questions.title: Reliable alerting logic
questions.prompt: Which description best fits a reliable alerting logic for this case?

options.text: It is enough if the list exists formally; open reachability gaps can be improvised in an emergency.
options.rationale: Improvisation does not replace a verified alerting sequence.

options.text: First contact, substitute, fallback and external partner contact are current, verifiable and clearly assigned to the incident type.
options.rationale: This combination makes alerting practically usable rather than merely formal.

options.text: Once service management is informed, the remaining alerting can be decided based on team availability.
options.rationale: The alert sequence must not depend on spontaneous availability.

options.text: The external hotline is more important than internal substitutes and should therefore be the sole focus.
options.rationale: External contacts are only one part of the overall alerting logic.

feedback.correct: The reliable alerting logic was correctly identified.
feedback.wrong: The target line includes current first contact, substitute, fallback and a suitable external reference.

questions.title: Build contact and escalation matrix
questions.subject: Prepare matrix for first contact, substitute and fallback
questions.body:
Hello everyone,

before I approve the list, I need a clear statement per alerting level on who is contacted first, who can substitute, how fallback works and which first evidence later shows that the contact sequence was tested or updated. Please combine internal and external contacts into one shared picture.

Kind regards
Emergency coordination

questions.title: Contact path and first evidence per alerting level
questions.prompt: For each alerting level, add the most likely contact path and the first appropriate evidence.

rows.label: First contact / contact path
rows.expected: Current on-call number of the responsible person with confirmed reachability.
options: Current on-call number of the responsible person with confirmed reachability.
Any earlier mobile number from an old incident log.
Only the team calendar without phone number.
A general mailbox address without delivery logic.

rows.label: First contact / evidence
rows.expected: Updated on-call list or successful test contact with date.
options: Updated on-call list or successful test contact with date.
Screenshot of the company logo in the alerting tool.
General weekly planning of the team.
Phone note without reference to the updated number.

rows: Substitute when first contact is unreachable

rows.label: Substitute / contact path
rows.expected: Named substitute with their own number and clear responsibility for the status.
options: Named substitute with their own number and clear responsibility for the status.
The assumption that someone from the platform team will react.
A chat room without named responsible persons.
Only the old mobile number of the primary person.

rows.label: Substitute / evidence
rows.expected: Documented substitute rule with contact path and review date.
options: Documented substitute rule with contact path and review date.
Only a rough team agreement without date reference.
Calendar invitation for a later meeting.
List of all employees without role reference.

rows.label: Partner contact / contact path
rows.expected: Current hotline or service contact from the valid partner contract with a fallback contact person.
options: Current hotline or service contact from the valid partner contract with a fallback contact person.
The former hotline from the expired contract as long as it appears in old documents.
A general contact form without reference to after-hours incidents.
Only the partner's corporate website.

rows.label: Partner contact / evidence
rows.expected: Updated contact info or verified test call with reference to the valid contract.
options: Updated contact info or verified test call with reference to the valid contract.
General supplier list without hotline details.
Photo of a partner visitor badge.
Short note that the partner used to be reachable.

feedback.correct: Contact path and first evidence were matched consistently per alerting level.
feedback.wrong: Check which contact path is truly reliable and which evidence can later support the update or test.

questions.title: Roles in on-call and escalation
questions.prompt: Which role takes which initial responsibility in this alerting case?

pairs.right: Consolidate alerting sequence, fallback and open gaps in the overall picture
Confirm on-call coverage, substitutes and reachability of internal roles as binding
Secure technically critical on-call roles and their substitutes for incidents
Update valid partner contact, hotline status and contract reference

options: Consolidate alerting sequence, fallback and open gaps in the overall picture
Confirm on-call coverage, substitutes and reachability of internal roles as binding
Secure technically critical on-call roles and their substitutes for incidents
Update valid partner contact, hotline status and contract reference

feedback.correct: The roles were matched with their initial responsibilities.
feedback.wrong: Focus on who owns the overall picture, internal reachability, technical readiness and partner contact.

questions.title: Open contact gaps before release
questions.prompt: The alert list contains 14 contact points. 8 are already verified, 3 were updated successfully today and 1 external contact is confirmed as valid. How many contact points remain open before the list can be released?

feedback.correct: The number of remaining open contact points was calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please subtract the already verified and updated contact points from the total.

questions.title: Secure closure for Friday
questions.subject: Prepare evidence picture for the alerting drill
questions.body:
Hello everyone,

for Friday I do not need a general on-call plan, but a readable status of which contact paths were updated, which substitutes are now binding, whether the drill worked after corrections and which residual points remain open. Please also make visible if external contacts could not yet be fully verified.

Kind regards
Audit

questions.title: Short status on alerting readiness
questions.prompt: Write a short note (4-6 sentences) with contact status, substitute logic, test result and open residual point.
questions.expectedOutline: Briefly describe what was updated, how substitute and fallback now run, what the test showed and which point remains open.

feedback.correct: The short status on alerting readiness is concise and reliable.
feedback.wrong: Please separate contact status, substitute logic, test result and residual point more clearly.

questions.title: Restore alerting status cleanly
questions.prompt: Put the steps in a sensible order so alerting becomes reliable after the drill.
items: Collect contact gaps, outdated numbers and unclear substitutes cleanly.
Update first contact, substitute, fallback and external partner contact.
Practically test or verify the corrected alerting sequence.
Document open residual points and unverified contacts visibly.
Release the closure picture for Friday with contact status and test result.
correctOrder: Collect contact gaps, outdated numbers and unclear substitutes cleanly.
Update first contact, substitute, fallback and external partner contact.
Practically test or verify the corrected alerting sequence.
Document open residual points and unverified contacts visibly.
Release the closure picture for Friday with contact status and test result.

feedback.correct: The order leads cleanly from gaps through correction to reliable closure.
feedback.wrong: First collect and correct gaps, then run the drill, only after that document residual points and closure.
